themes.topic: Samsung Q2 Earnings Beat on AI Memory Demand
themes.summary: Samsung Electronics reported a significant Q2 operating profit beat, surging 19x year-over-year to ~$58.4B, driven by strong AI memory demand, despite revenue slightly missing consensus.

mentions.text:
$005930 (Bloomberg) -- Samsung reported operating profit for the second quarter that beat the average analyst estimate.
SECOND QUARTER RESULTS
•Operating profit 89.40 trillion won, estimate 84.2 trillion won (Bloomberg Consensus)
•Sales 171.00 trillion won, estimate 169.23 trilli

*SAMSUNG 2Q OPER PROFIT 89.40T WON, EST. 84.2T WON

Samsung’s quarterly profit up 19X YoY, beating estimates. https://t.co/yTszuSJilQ

2026.07.07 07:39:44
Company: Samsung Electronics (Market cap: KRW 1,859.1tn) A005930
Report title: Preliminary Earnings Based on Consolidated Financial Statements / Fair Disclosure
Revenue: KRW 171.0tn (Consensus: KRW 173.0tn / -1%)
Operating profit: KRW 89.4tn (Consensus: KRW 84

Samsung Electronics prelim Q2 operating profit surged 19x YoY to ~$58.4B, above $55.3B consensus, on AI memory demand.

However, prelim Q2 revenue came in slightly shy at ~$111.8B, vs $113.1B consensus, though still up 129% YoY. https://t.co/7YFzWAOSMl

Samsung earnings call going to be fire tonight! 

Pictured: Average Korean man dialling into the Samsung earnings call https://t.co/H2JQYQTgmO

themes.topic: NVIDIA Refutes Kyber Delay Claims & CPO/Optical Debates
themes.summary: NVIDIA has refuted claims of Kyber project delays, stating its roadmap is intact, amidst ongoing market debate about CPO delays and the technical merits of VCSEL versus ELS for optical interconnects in AI infrastructure.

mentions.text:
This week I am putting myself in the bears shoes...

I am bullish because I get as familiar as possible with the legitimate bull cases and work through them

And acknowledge when they are real and need to be watched

So a few things I'm working on: 

1 - Oversupply concerns with

$NVDA refuting recent Kyber delay claims:

“Our roadmap is intact”.

lol, damage has already been done with stocks in many markets (esp. in Taiwan/Japan/Soutj Korea) crashing after the “report” https://t.co/ijHggRm1fu

Do not be misled by SemiAnalysis reports designed to manipulate the market.

Look at the fundamental https://t.co/lNipNqgQ6o

So true. 

It’s wild how the market panics over minor timeline shifts, ignoring the engineering realities. 

When $NVDA is inventing the state of the art and constantly redrawing the boundaries, execution hurdles are just part of the process.

To be blunt, some analysts jumped the gun by baking experimental tech into their models way too fast. The reality check is that orthogonal backplanes are hitting a brick wall—neither M9 nor PTFE can pass the validation tests right now.

themes.topic: Humanoid Robotics Adoption Driven by Global Labor Scarcity
themes.summary: Widespread humanoid robotics adoption is anticipated due to severe global labor scarcity and rapidly falling robot costs, with companies like Agility Robotics and Amazon conducting trials, leading to compressed payback periods.

themes.topic: Reddit's AI Data Monetization & Stock Recovery
themes.summary: Reddit ($RDDT) is experiencing a stock recovery, demonstrating profitability and growth as AI companies become significant customers for its data, transforming a perceived threat into a vital revenue stream.

mentions.text:
Was a sad few months for $RDDT. But glad it's finally back above $200. 

Reddit was doing:
- $663M revenue w/ 91.5% gross margins
- $204M GAAP net income
- 45%+ fwd Y/Y growth after 69%+ growth.
- Net profit is ~30.7% of revenue

Felt very weird to see a profitable company get dr

The whole thesis this year was AI kills every software stock. $RDDT did the opposite.

One of the few names that's actually profitable AND still growing fast

And the funny part is AI was supposed to be the threat. reddit pulled $311M in free cash flow on $1M of capex last quarte

themes.topic: Samsung CXL 3.1 Memory Production Delayed by CPU Launches
themes.summary: Samsung Electronics has delayed the mass production of its CXL 3.1 memory modules due to postponed launches of supporting CPUs from Intel and AMD, and a slow buildout of the CXL 3.1 ecosystem.

mentions.text:
Samsung Electronics Delays CXL 3.1 Memory Mass Production
